items.title: 'Terrifying' experience for WA family as storm tears roof off their home

children.content: A woman describes her "terrifying" ordeal after a powerful cold front which sounded like a "jet plane" tore the roof off her family home while she was inside it on Monday night.

gallery.altText: Letterbox reading '10' in the foreground with a gutted home in the background.
A brick home with damaged roof and front window

thumbnail.altText: Letterbox reading '10' in the foreground with a gutted home in the background.
A brick home with damaged roof and front window

items.title: Rates bill shock forecast as councils struggle with debt

image.altText: A report on a wooden desk top is titled "Local Government 2025" with "OAG" in the top corner.

children.content: Local governments in WA are increasingly unable to pay their bills amid growing levels of debt, according to a financial health scorecard — and the result could be higher rates and delays to projects.

items.title: Workers to be axed, production slowed at one of WA's last coal mines

children.content: Premier Coal is set to cut jobs and production at its Collie operation, citing slowing demand for coal-fired power as a key factor.

gallery.altText: A long shot of a coal mine with a large truck in shot.
A long shot of a coal mine.

thumbnail.altText: A long shot of a coal mine with a large truck in shot.
A long shot of a coal mine.

items.title: Judge condemns 'outrageous' school stabbing planned on Snapchat

children.content: Two boys are sentenced for their roles in the stabbing of a 15-year-old boy at a Perth high school, offending the prosecutor said was part of a "game" formulated online.

image.altText: A yellow Snapchat app logo on a black smart phone.

items.title: Pressure on Department of Justice after second woman dies at Perth prison

children.content: Calls are mounting for a "transparent" government review of WA's Bandyup Women's Prison after a second Aboriginal inmate dies inside the facility in less than five weeks.

image.altText: A sign out the front of Bandyup Women's Prison

items.title: 'Outrageous mess': About 2,000 AI road safety camera fines withdrawn in WA

children.content: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

gallery.altText: A screenshot of a fine issues for a seatbelt issue

thumbnail.altText: A screenshot of a fine issues for a seatbelt issue

items.title: Last chance for answers as police admit error in search for Nannup Four

children.content: The family of a mother and daughter who went missing, along with two others, in WA almost 20 years ago are pinning their hopes on a last-ditch effort to uncover the truth of what became of them.

gallery.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

thumbnail.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

items.title: Four additional diesel cargoes secured under government's underwriting powers

children.content: Anthony Albanese secures four additional cargoes of diesel for Australia via BP, Viva Energy and Export Finance Australia. Follow live.

items.title: In a split second, an abuse survivor made the 'surreal' decision to talk

children.content: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

image.altText: a woman with clasped hands on her lap

items.title: Closure of specialist centres for children with autism puts spotlight on NDIS funding

children.content: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

articles.title: 500,000 Australians wait for end to 'limbo' as Butler readies NDIS plan

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

image.altText: Two men in dark suits enter the lower house.

articles.title: Where does all the NDIS money go and how did we get here?

children.content: With more changes surrounding the NDIS set to be announced, here’s everything you need to know about the scheme.

image.altText: A graphic showing a child running, an NDIS logo, a calculator and arrows on a green background

items.title: 'Large crocodile' attacks man in WA's far north

children.content: A man in his 20s has been flown to a Perth hospital after he was attacked by a crocodile at a popular camping spot north of Broome.

items.title: 'It's been horrible': Your experiences with family estrangement

children.content: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

items.title: E-bike rider dies after colliding with public bus in Perth's north-east

children.content: A 50-year-old man dies at the scene after his e-bike is involved in a crash with a Transperth bus at an intersection in Swan View, with police appealing for witnesses.

gallery.altText: Blue and white tape across a road
A junk food advertisement for an orange drink from Hungry Jacks on the side of a Transperth bus as it travels along a road.

items.title: Residents urged to stay in as burnt battery product may react with forecast rain

children.content: Emergency services say a fire at the Li-ion Energy site in Maddington in Perth's south-east  — the biggest blaze of its kind in WA's history — will be reviewed to inform future responses to lithium battery fires.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: Travel quiz: Grab your bags and see if you can beat our quiz

children.content: Which city is hosting the first game of the World Cup? What's the name of Egypt's currency?

image.altText: A few dozen people swim in beautiful blue water giving off steam and mist in front of a picturesque mountain range.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

items.title: Eczema medication costing families thousands due to PBS age limit

children.content: The cost of relief for kids under 12 with severe eczema is $1,600 a month, but a push for PBS funding is looking to change that.

image.altText: A boy sits eating an ice block with bandages around his arms and legs.

items.title: Why this man risked crocodiles, storms and jail to fish in Australian waters

children.content: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

gallery.altText: A man in a green shirt looks to the right.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green shirt looks to the right.

items.title: Father calls for accountability over 21-month-old son's death

children.content: The father of Sandipan Dhar, who died after not receiving a blood test at Joondalup Health Campus, says there is still a lack of accountability over his son's death.

image.altText: A boy dressed in winter clothes sitting on a black couch

items.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child

children.content: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

image.altText: Barry Cable walks up the stairs to court followed by a media pack.

items.title: 'Finding hidden gems' in 100 years of soil stored at national archive

children.content: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

image.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

items.title: Tow truck driver weeps after sentencing for fatal crash

children.content: Ross David Gamage has been fined $5,000 and had his licence suspended for six months over a 2023 crash that killed a 45-year-old man near Waroona.

image.altText: Rpss Gamage walks out of the Bunbury Courthouse after the decision.

items.title: Prospector couldn't believe his luck when he found rare australite

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.
